_description: Target companies for direct ATS board polling. Primary discovery source — preferred over WebSearch because ATS JSON endpoints return structured, fresh, deduplicated data with no 403/JS issues.

_websearch_sources.description: Supplemental discovery sources run via WebSearch each daily pipeline run. Use these to find companies NOT on the ATS watchlist, especially Atlanta startups.

sources.name: Hypepotamus Job Board
sources.disabled_reason: Job listings are JavaScript-rendered via nonce-protected AJAX. Direct WebFetch returns empty shell; WP REST API endpoints for listings are not public. Cannot be polled headlessly without a browser dependency. Tested 2026-05-08.

sources.name: BuiltIn Atlanta
sources.query: site:builtin.com/job ("customer success" OR "technical account" OR "implementation" OR "solutions engineer" OR "support operations" OR "customer operations" OR "deployment") Atlanta
sources.notes: Atlanta tech companies, mid-size focus. Atlanta small company bonus (+20) applies for HQ Atlanta headcount ≤200. Query broadened 2026-06-25 to include customer success + solutions engineer terms.

sources.name: BuiltIn Remote
sources.query: site:builtin.com/job ("customer success" OR "technical account" OR "implementation" OR "solutions engineer" OR "support operations" OR "customer operations" OR "deployment") remote
sources.notes: Remote roles from tech companies nationally. No Atlanta bonus — needs stronger title/keyword fit to clear threshold. Salary floor ($100K) still applies. Query broadened 2026-06-25.

sources.name: Wellfound Startups
sources.query: site:wellfound.com ("customer success" OR "technical account" OR implementation OR "solutions engineer" OR deployment OR "support operations" OR "customer operations") startup remote OR Atlanta
sources.notes: Formerly AngelList. Early-stage startups nationally (Series A/B focus). Atlanta HQ companies still get +20 bonus. Remote roles score on fit alone. Skews equity-heavy — verify salary floor before tailoring. Skip pre-seed if comp not listed. Query broadened 2026-06-25.

sources.name: AI-Titled Roles (Broad Discovery)
sources.query: ("AI Success Manager" OR "AI Outcomes Manager" OR "AI Adoption" OR "AI Transformation Lead" OR "AI Value Partner" OR "AI Engagement Lead" OR "AI Delivery Manager" OR "AI GTM") jobs remote 2026
sources.notes: Catch-all WebSearch for novel AI-prefixed role titles that don't match named tiers. Companies are still coining these. Run every pipeline pass. Apply AI/LLM bonus (+20) to any hits. Check for salary floor before surfacing. Score against tier2b_ai_wildcard (+18) if no higher tier matches.
sources.scoring_notes: Apply +20 AI bonus + tier2b_ai_wildcard title match (+18) = strong signal. Verify salary floor.

sources.name: Ashby Boards - Target Roles
sources.query: site:jobs.ashbyhq.com ("customer success" OR "technical account" OR "implementation" OR "solutions consultant" OR "deployment" OR "professional services" OR "customer engineer") 2026
sources.notes: Discovers Ashby-hosted companies NOT on the watchlist. Returns ~10 results; scan for UNFAMILIAR company names (skip ones already on the watchlist). When a new company appears, verify its board (pipeline/verify_workday.py pattern or a direct Ashby API check) and add it to the watchlist so the poller scans its full roster going forward. Added 2026-06-25.

sources.name: Greenhouse Boards - Target Roles
sources.query: site:job-boards.greenhouse.io ("technical account manager" OR "customer success manager" OR "implementation" OR "solutions engineer" OR "deployment" OR "professional services") 2026
sources.notes: Discovers Greenhouse-hosted companies off the watchlist. Same enroll guidance as Ashby: surface unfamiliar companies, verify board, add to watchlist. Added 2026-06-25.

sources.name: Lever Boards - Target Roles
sources.query: site:jobs.lever.co ("technical account manager" OR "customer success manager" OR "implementation" OR "solutions consultant" OR "deployment") 2026
sources.notes: Discovers Lever-hosted companies off the watchlist. Same enroll guidance. Added 2026-06-25.

sources.name: AI-Native & AI-Safety Orgs (vertical discovery)
sources.query: ("AI safety" OR "frontier model" OR "applied AI" OR "AI research" OR "contact center AI" OR "customer support AI") ("customer success" OR operations OR implementation OR deployment OR solutions) jobs 2026
sources.notes: Catches AI-native and AI-safety research orgs (e.g. FAR.AI-type) that are off the watchlist and whose fitting roles may be titled differently (e.g. 'Operations Generalist'). Vertical/company discovery, not title-keyed — surface the COMPANY, then verify board + add to watchlist so the poller scans its full roster. Apply AI bonus (+20) to in-vertical hits. Added 2026-06-25 after a manually-found FAR.AI role exposed that the discovery layer was too title-narrow.

_workday_notes: Workday entries use ats='workday' and carry wd_host (incl. wdN datacenter), wd_tenant, and wd_site instead of relying on slug for the endpoint (slug is still set = tenant, for dedup-key prefixing). The public careers page is JS-rendered; poll_ats.py POSTs to the internal CXS JSON API instead. Salary is NOT in the list response (it's on each job's detail page), so the poller treats it as neutral and Claude pulls exact salary + posting date from the JD at tailoring time, where the salary-floor and 21-day freshness gates are applied. wd_host/wd_tenant/wd_site are NOT guessable per company - verify new tenants with pipeline/verify_workday.py before adding (find the exact careers URL first, e.g. via 'site:myworkdayjobs.com [company]').

_scoring_config.notes_near_miss: Near-misses are listed (with links) in the email digest but NOT tailored, so Aneesh can eyeball them. Two kinds, unioned: (A) score near-miss — passes all hard filters incl. salary, but scores below light_tailoring_threshold (no lower score bound, capture all). (B) salary near-miss — passes all other filters but salary midpoint is below salary_floor_usd yet at or above near_miss_salary_floor_usd. See SKILL Step 2 + Step 5.
_scoring_config.notes_salary: Compare the MIDPOINT of a listed base-salary range against salary_floor_usd — never the bottom or top. Single figure: use as-is. OTE-only: estimate base (80% for variable/sales roles, 100% otherwise). No salary listed: do not filter, treat as neutral in scoring. See SKILL Step 2b for the authoritative rule.
_scoring_config.notes_freshness: Add +10 for postings ≤2 days old, +2 for ≤7 days. Validated by empirical data: response rates fall sharply after 48 hours.
_scoring_config.notes: Light tailoring for IC-level roles 78-87. Full tailoring for 88+. Skip below 78. Capped companies require 110+ regardless of tier. Cap rule: count entries where applied=true AND outcome=null (pending applications). If that count ≥ company_cap_max_applied_pending, new roles need company_cap_threshold to surface. Queued/unapplied roles do NOT count toward the cap — only submitted applications do.

_title_scoring_tiers.description: Aneesh's background is Technical Support Operations Manager — he runs a support function end-to-end: hiring/training agents, AI deployment, knowledge base, Salesforce administration, QA auditing, BPO management. Score title match accordingly. Operations-first and implementation-first roles are true matches; pure relationship CSM is a stretch.

tier3_reasonable_stretch.notes: CSM roles are structurally different (account/relationship ownership vs. operations leadership) but viable when the JD emphasizes technical depth, deployment, or team management.

tier4_weak_stretch.notes: Only worth surfacing if AI bonus, Atlanta bonus, or salary pushes score above 78 threshold.

tier2b_ai_wildcard.description: Catch-all for novel AI-prefixed roles companies are coining in 2025-2026. Apply when a title is NOT matched by tier1 or tier2 BUT satisfies: (1) 'AI' appears as a word-boundary token, AND (2) title contains ANY of: customer, deployment, implementation, engagement, outcomes, adoption, success, transformation, strategy, value, delivery, operations, solutions. Score as +18 (between T2 +22 and T3 +15). Flag for manual review in email digest. Do NOT apply to 'AI Engineer' or 'AI Architect' titles (those are IC/coding roles).
